stories.title: Build v2 infrastructure: storage abstraction, agent bus, and workflow graph generator
acceptance_criteria: store.py provides AbstractStore with LocalStore implementation and file-lock support
agent_bus.py produces JSONL events and Markdown discussion logs per wave
workflow_graph.py generates correct Mermaid and Graphviz output from task.json

stories.title: Build v2 core engine: async orchestrator and FastAPI backend
acceptance_criteria: zeus_orchestrator.py dispatches 3+ tasks concurrently within a wave without corrupting task.json
Orchestrator uses asyncio.Semaphore and isolated agent workspaces
zeus_server.py exposes /status, /events, /discussion, /graph/mermaid endpoints

stories.title: Deliver zero-build Web UI dashboard
acceptance_criteria: Web frontend (index.html) renders dashboard and graph without npm/build tools
Web UI consumes the same FastAPI backend

stories.title: Integrate, test, document, and validate backward compatibility
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests pass for end-to-end 2-wave execution with mock agents
zeus_runner.py --status on main remains unchanged and functional
v2 documentation (zeus-execute-v2.md) and README updates are complete

stories.title: Adaptive Wave Rescheduling — maximize concurrency slot utilization
acceptance_criteria: task.json schema supports original_wave, scheduled_wave, and rescheduled_from
zeus_orchestrator.py uses a priority queue and fills empty slots from future waves when current wave tasks block
Agent Bus emits task.rescheduled events for every dynamic move
Web UI correctly displays rescheduled tasks and updated wave progress
Tests confirm backward compatibility and state consistency under concurrent updates

stories.title: Subagent Dispatcher Integration — enable true unattended multi-agent execution
acceptance_criteria: subagent_dispatcher.py provides SubagentDispatcher ABC and implementations for mock, kimi, claude, and auto
zeus_orchestrator.py delegates task execution to the dispatcher without leaking platform specifics
config.json optionally accepts subagent.dispatcher and subagent.timeout_seconds
Unit tests cover command construction, auto-selection, and timeout behavior
Web UI Agent Monitor shows activity indicators for running agents
Documentation (zeus-execute-v2.md, README) explains how to enable Kimi or Claude subagent mode

stories.title: Subagent Workspace Bootstrap — auto-inject identity and context files
acceptance_criteria: zeus_orchestrator.py copies AGENTS.md, USER.md, IDENTITY.md, and SOUL.md into every agent workspace before dispatch
config.json optionally accepts subagent.bootstrap.files to customize the list
Missing files are skipped without raising exceptions
A task.bootstrapped event is emitted for observability
Tests cover default behavior and config override
Documentation (zeus-execute-v2.md) explains the bootstrap behavior and config option

stories.title: Web UI Improvements & Multi-language Support
acceptance_criteria: Web UI provides a Milestones tab that groups tasks by roadmap milestone with per-milestone progress bars
Task schema supports optional title_en, title_zh, description_en, description_zh
Web UI has a language toggle (EN/ZH) and all task displays are localized with graceful fallback
All existing tests pass and new tests cover the /milestones endpoint
i18n fields are seeded for all existing v2 tasks and documented in zeus-execute-v2.md

stories.title: Phase Layer — Delivery Batch Management for Wave Proliferation
acceptance_criteria: roadmap.json schema supports an optional phases array with title, summary, milestone_ids, and wave_range
GET /phases endpoint returns phases with computed status, progress_percent, and nested milestones
Web UI shows a Phases tab with summary-bearing phase cards and phase-aware wave selector
task.json optionally tracks meta.current_phase
All tests pass and new tests cover /phases and phase status calculation
Documentation explains how to define phases in roadmap.json
